items.summary: ASML is set to make around a fifth of its 2026 net sales from China, but geopolitical headwinds present a delicate situation.

items.title: India's forex reserves rise $964 million to $675.16 billion for week ended July 10
items.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves increased by $964 million to $675.16 billion. Foreign currency assets, the largest component, grew by $930 million during the week. Gold reserves also recorded an increase, rising by $24 million to $105.23 billion. Special Drawing Rights and reserve tranche position with the IMF saw minor increases. Earlier this year, reserves had touched a record high of $728.494 billion.

items.title: The financial winners and losers from the World Cup
items.summary: Big bucks are being made from the 2026 tournament off the field, but who is raking in the most, and who is losing out?

items.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
items.summary: Indian markets ended the week strongly as Sensex surged 964 points and Nifty gained 262 points, driven by robust Q1 earnings and IT buying. Analysts see improving momentum in large caps, with Nifty's breakout indicating potential upside towards 24,800 despite broader market weakness.

items.title: Markets defy global gloom; crude tops $85
items.summary: The Nifty 50 rose 1.09%, or 262 points, to settle at 24,334, while the Sensex advanced 1.25% to close at 78,151

items.title: Oil prices rise as U.S.-Iran hostilities threaten supply through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices rose Friday as investors weighed escalating threats between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: West Asia crisis, uncertain monsoon major risks for growth: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra noted West Asia crisis and weak monsoon pose growth risks. India's economy grew over seven percent recently, supported by strong fundamentals. The central bank projects 6.6 percent GDP growth for the current financial year. Inflation forecasts are raised to 5.1 percent, driven by supply-side factors. The rupee remains stable globally, and FDI inflows are robust.

items.title: A $5 trillion idea to save energy is hiding in plain sight
items.summary: Energy efficiency offers a simple, effective solution to rising global energy demands. This approach can create trillions in economic value and millions of jobs. It also significantly reduces climate pollution and premature deaths from air quality. Government policies and programs are essential for achieving these substantial energy savings.

items.title: Federal Bank among 4 stocks that hit 52-week highs and rallied up to 25% in a month
items.summary: Sensex ended 96 points higher on Friday as four BSE 200 stocks—Oracle Financial Services, Adani Energy Solutions, BHEL and Federal Bank—hit fresh 52-week highs. The milestone reflects sustained buying interest, improving investor sentiment and continued bullish momentum in these counters.

items.title: JSW One Platforms IPO: JSW Steel to raise up to ₹811 crore through stake sale
items.summary: Board approves participation as promoter selling shareholder; Q1 net profit at ₹4,696 crore

items.title: Trump doubles down on 2020 election claims in national address, alleging China meddling
items.summary: Trump also repeated his calls for the Republican-led Congress to pass the "SAVE America Act," a controversial election bill that lacks support to pass.

items.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
items.summary: The UK government said taking the firm into public hands would safeguard "a vital national capability".

items.title: I’m suddenly not loving my UK shares like I used to
items.summary: Global gyrations in stock prices and earnings favour other markets

items.title: Shapoorji Pallonji Group completes long-delayed debt fundraising, sources say
items.summary: The Shapoorji Pallonji Group has successfully wrapped up its fundraising campaign, garnering bids amounting to a remarkable 215 billion rupees for a three-year bond. This initiative is primarily designed to refinance the conglomerate's existing debts while investors have been reassured about the monetization of its stake in Tata Sons. Notably, a Mauritius-based entity played a crucial role in the rupee tranche investment.

items.title: JSW Steel to raise ₹811 crore through JSW One IPO
items.summary: JSW Steel will sell shares as promoter shareholder in the proposed listing of its B2B commerce platform, which crossed the $1 billion valuation mark last year.

items.title: India's development did not come to standstill despite oil supply crisis: PM Modi
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday said India's railway network would have faced a major crisis during the recent West Asia conflict if it had remained dependent on diesel, highlighting that nearly 99% of Indian Railways has been electrified over the past 12 years.

items.title: Crisil Ratings upgrades long-term ratings of Vedanta group companies
items.summary: Crisil Ratings has upgraded Vedanta group companies' long-term ratings to AA+ and assigned a stable outlook. This upgrade reflects the strong financial profile and improved financial flexibility of the demerged entities. Vedanta Ltd anchors the group with market leadership and a significant stake in Hindustan Zinc. Leverage is expected to remain comfortably below 1.0x over the medium term. The group's diversified metals portfolio strengthens its overall operating profile.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps 965 pts, Nifty ends above 24,334; financials rally ahead of key earnings
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Sensex settled 964.58 pts or 1.25% higher at 78,151.45; and Nifty 50 climbed 261.55 pts or 1.09% to 24,334.30.

items.title: Pirates allegedly hijack chemical tanker off Yemen coast in Gulf of Aden: Report
items.summary: Armed assailants boarded the chemical tanker Asana in the Gulf of Aden off southern Yemen on Friday and are believed to have taken control of the vessel, according to maritime security sources.

items.title: EPFO launches six-month 'VISHWAS 2026' scheme to settle employer penalty disputes, promote compliance
items.summary: The Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) has launched 'VISHWAS, 2026', a one-time dispute resolution scheme aimed at encouraging employers to settle long-pending damages and penalty-related cases through a simplified digital process, while promoting voluntary compliance and reducing litigation.

items.title: Oberoi Realty Q1 profit jumps 29% to ₹544 crore, revenue rises 32%; declares ₹2 interim dividend
items.summary: Oberoi Realty reported Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹544 crore, up 29% YoY. Revenue rose 31.7% to ₹1,301 crore, with EBITDA increasing 41% to ₹734 crore. The company also declared an interim dividend of ₹2 per share as it continues to see strong demand for luxury homes.

items.title: Jio Financial shares surge 3% as Q1 profit jumps 156% y-o-y
items.summary: Its consolidated net profit for Q1FY27 rallied 156 per cent y-o-y to ₹830 crore.

items.title: PB Fintech share price rises 3% after HDFC Mutual Fund stake raise to 5.02%
items.summary: PB Fintech shares rose 3% intraday on July 17 after HDFC Mutual Fund increased its stake to 5.02% via an open market purchase. The fund acquired 129,068 shares, raising its holding slightly above the 5% threshold. Shares were bought through various mutual fund schemes.

items.title: EQT boosted by strong fundraising momentum and earnings growth
items.summary: Shares rally 13% as private equity group’s fee-generating assets under management beat expectations

items.title: CAFE III norms signal faster EV shift in India while balancing automakers concerns: Nomura
items.summary: CAFE refers to government regulations that require automakers to meet a specific average level of fuel economy or CO₂ emissions across their entire fleet of vehicles sold in a year. Under the draft notification, compliance will be evaluated over FY28-30 and FY30-32, giving manufacturers additional time to phase in electric vehicle (EV) launches.

items.title: JSW Steel Q1 result: Net Profit doubles to ₹4,696 crore; revenue jumps 10%
items.summary: JSW Steel reported a Q1 FY27 net profit of ₹4,696 crore, more than double last year’s ₹2,209 crore, driven by a 9.8% revenue increase to ₹47,364 crore. The company began construction on a new 2-million-tonne steel plant in Andhra Pradesh as part of its expansion.

items.title: SBI Capital Markets, HDFC Bank among 4 book runners appointed for NLC India Renewables IPO
items.summary: The proposed IPO is expected to strengthen NIRL's capital base and support its long-term growth strategy in the renewable energy sector

items.title: Rupee logs sharpest weekly drop in nine as oil price jump stings
items.summary: The rupee closed at 96.28 per dollar, down about 1% week-on-week, due to a 13% rise in Brent crude oil prices

items.title: Rupee closes at 96.28 vs USD; logs sharpest weekly drop since May as oil price jump stings
items.summary: The Indian rupee faced its most significant weekly drop since May, driven by rising oil prices and strong demand for the dollar. Increasing tensions between the U.S. and Iran exacerbated concerns over energy supplies, further straining the rupee's position. However, interventions from state-run banks provided some relief, temporarily halting its downward trend. Market participants are closely monitoring oil prices and the Reserve Bank of India’s approach to the rupee’s depreciation.

items.title: Reliance Industries Q1 Results Live: RIL to announce earnings today; EBITDA may rise up to 10% on O2C, Jio growth
Rs 9,000 crore bet! Reliance Industries promoters increase stake by 0.5% in June quarter
items.summary: Reliance Industries’ promoters increased their stake to 50.48% in the June quarter through market purchases worth an estimated Rs 8,500–9,000 crore. The move signals confidence despite the stock’s weak performance, earnings misses and challenges across retail and oil-to-chemicals businesses.

items.title: India's smartphone shipments fall 10% in Q2, biggest June-quarter drop in six years
items.summary: India's smartphone shipments saw a significant ten percent decline year-on-year. Rising memory prices forced brands to increase handset prices, impacting demand. The budget segment experienced a substantial forty-five percent drop in shipments. Samsung was the only major brand to achieve shipment growth during this period.

items.title: Hang Seng crashes over 2% as tech selloff hits Asia markets. Why Hong Kong stocks are falling on Friday?
items.summary: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2% by midday on Friday, weighed down by a global selloff in technology stocks as renewed inflation concerns dampened risk appetite. The Hang Seng Tech Index plunged 4%, with investors reducing exposure to technology and other growth stocks amid fears that higher inflation could lead to tighter monetary policy and higher interest rates.

items.title: Fighter jet maker Saab smashes expectations as CEO urges procurement rethink
items.summary: European governments ramp up defense spending and book orders with the region's companies.

items.title: Tata Power to declare Q1 results 2026 on this date. Check details
items.summary: Tata Power's Board of Directors will convene on July 27 to review Q1 FY27 financial results. The trading window has been closed since June 24.

items.title: SpaceX falls further in premarket after Starship test flight aborted
items.summary: SpaceX's stock plunged further on Friday, a day after it aborted a test flight for its Starship rocket at the last second.

items.title: Global Market: South Korea's AI-driven stock frenzy fuels volatility, challenges market fundamentals
items.summary: The sharp swings have unsettled investors and regulators alike, with market movements increasingly driven by leveraged investment products rather than corporate fundamentals. The volatility has also complicated the assessment of South Korean equities, particularly as the country remains a critical supplier of AI-related semiconductor technology.

items.title: Global Market: Euro zone current account surplus widens in May; market impact likely limited
items.summary: The euro zone's current account surplus increased in May, driven by stronger primary income. This rise offset a narrower trade surplus, according to European Central Bank data. On an unadjusted basis, a deficit was recorded for the month. Over twelve months, the surplus represented 1.7% of gross domestic product. Stock markets are not expected to react significantly to this data.

items.title: Bitcoin falls nearly 2% to $63,000 as geopolitical tensions weigh on crypto markets
items.summary: Ethereum fell 3.98% in the past 24 hours to trade at the $1,828 mark. Among the major altcoins, BNB, XRP, Solana, Tron, Hyperliquid, Dogecoin, and Cardano corrected up to 11.31%.

items.title: JSW Steel Q1 Results: Net profit doubles to Rs 4,696 crore
items.summary: JSW Steel has showcased impressive financial growth in the first quarter of the fiscal year, with its net profit skyrocketing to Rs 4,696 crore, a significant climb compared to previous figures. Total income surged to Rs 48,088 crore, a rise from Rs 43,497 crore last year. Additionally, crude steel production grew by three percent in the June quarter, as the company initiated a new steel plant project in Andhra Pradesh.

items.title: Why has British Steel been nationalised?
items.summary: The UK's only remaining plant making virgin steel has been taken into public ownership after years of uncertainty over its future.

items.title: Nothing leads India's smartphone growth in Q2 as shipments surge 105%: Report
items.summary: Nothing led India's smartphone market growth in the second quarter of 2026. This surge followed strong demand for their Phone (4a) and Phone (4a) Pro models. Increased brand visibility came from sponsoring the Royal Challengers Bengaluru IPL franchise. Samsung also saw growth, while the overall market faces price challenges. Nothing attributes its success to product design and innovation in a difficult market.

items.title: Volkswagen layoffs: Workers look to grill CEO during plant visits over threat to 1,40,000 jobs — Here's what we know
items.summary: Volkswagen's works council will hold staff assemblies in August with CEO Oliver Blume to discuss plans risking up to 1,40,000 job losses. The meets will be at the German automaker's headquarters in Wolfsburg and at plants in Emden and Zwickau, which are at threat of closure.

items.title: Shyam Steel to invest Rs 15,000 crore in West Bengal expansion
items.summary: Shyam Steel Group will invest Rs 15,000 crores in West Bengal's expansion projects. This initiative will create over 20,000 direct and indirect employment opportunities. The company plans to establish a new integrated steel plant and other strategic sector investments. These developments aim to boost regional infrastructure and economic growth significantly.

items.title: Who is the frontrunner to be the UK's next chancellor?
items.summary: Andy Burnham will be moving into Downing Street next week, and a question remains over who will be his neighbour in Number 11.

items.title: This beaten-down fintech stock jumps 39% this week, on track for biggest weekly gain in nearly 5 years
items.summary: Fino Payments Bank's shares surged 14% to ₹180.75 on July 17, marking a potential weekly gain of 39%. Despite recovery, the stock remains down 34% this year, following a strong June update and increased customer engagement, though transaction throughput declined by 35%.

items.title: Jindal Stainless supplies 40% stainless steel for India's first hydrogen-powered train
items.summary: Jindal Stainless on Friday said it supplied about 40% of the stainless steel used in India's first hydrogen-powered train, flagged off by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

items.title: India launches first hydrogen train connecting Jind to Sonipat, a 'Make in India' success
items.summary: India launched its first hydrogen-powered train connecting Jind to Sonipat. This initiative marks a step towards clean and sustainable mobility in railways,. The train utilises hydrogen fuel cell technology, producing only water vapor as a byproduct. Prime Minister Narendra Modi hailed the launch as a success of the 'Make in India' campaign. This development positions India among countries operating such advanced train technology.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Why Facebook's copyright system is under scrutiny in Delhi HC
items.summary: Two creators allege rivals copied their videos, manipulated Facebook's “Edit Post” feature and used Meta's copyright enforcement system against them. Here's what the cases mean for content ownership and creator protection.

items.title: Foreign inflows in Asian bonds surge to seven-month high in June
items.summary: Asian bonds saw their largest foreign inflows in seven months during June. Lower oil prices and strong technology demand improved the region's economic outlook. Investors bought over eleven billion dollars in regional bonds, seeking safer fixed-income assets. South Korean and Indian bonds recorded substantial net foreign purchases. Indonesian bonds attracted significant cross-border inflows, driven by attractive yields.

items.title: Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveils Kimi model it says rivals OpenAI, Anthropic
items.summary: It's the latest AI model from China to close the performance gap with leading U.S. AI labs.

items.title: Zepto said to face sharply lower valuation for IPO
items.summary: Zepto Ltd. seeks a lower IPO valuation, reflecting industry cash burn concerns. Foreign investors indicate interest at $4.5 billion, while domestic ones value it lower. This contrasts sharply with its $7 billion valuation from last October's fundraise. The company aims to raise $850 million through its initial public offering. Proceeds will fund network expansion and technological infrastructure improvements.

items.title: Novo Nordisk's obesity drug Wegovy wins India approval to fight silent fatty liver killer
items.summary: India's drug regulator approved Novo Nordisk's Wegovy for MASH treatment. This drug targets a serious progressive form of fatty liver disease. The approval follows a committee recommendation for adults with moderate to advanced liver fibrosis. Wegovy, containing semaglutide, was previously approved for chronic weight management. This marks a significant expansion for the blockbuster obesity drug in India.

items.title: Nifty breaks past 24,200 as IT stocks surge; broader market lags
items.summary: Tech Mahindra leads Nifty 50 gainers, rising 4.10% to ₹1,572.20

items.title: After record $29 billion selloff, FIIs buying India again. Chris Wood explains why
items.summary: After a record $29 billion foreign selloff, FIIs have turned net buyers of Indian equities in July. Jefferies' Christopher Wood says the earlier outflows were driven by the AI-led rally in Korea and Taiwan, adding that sustained inflows will depend on the AI trade cooling or Indian valuations becoming more attractive.

items.title: India gets its first hydrogen train, but bigger story lies elsewhere
items.summary: India's first hydrogen-powered train, launched on the Jind-Sonipat route in Haryana, is less about transforming rail transport and more about showcasing the country's broader green hydrogen ambitions.

items.title: Central Bank of India Q1 results: Profit rises 13% to Rs 1,324 cr
items.summary: Central Bank of India announced a thirteen percent net profit increase for the June quarter. Total income rose to Rs 10,678 crore, and interest earned improved significantly. However, the bank's operating profit saw a decline from the previous year. Asset quality improved as gross non-performing assets moderated to two point six zero percent. The capital adequacy ratio also strengthened to eighteen point two eight percent during this period.

items.title: BHEL's profit comeback is real. Can its order boom sustain the rally?
items.summary: BHEL's first Q1 profit in seven years, stronger execution and a ₹2.6 trillion order book have fuelled a sharp turnaround. But rising competition and a 45x valuation could test the rally.

items.title: Global Market: European shares fall as tech selloff, Middle East tensions weigh; Saab gains
items.summary: European shares opened lower on Friday, heading for weekly losses. Escalating Middle East tensions and a tech stock selloff dampened investor sentiment. Technology stocks led the declines, dropping significantly across global markets. Investors shifted focus toward lagging sectors like luxury goods, which saw gains. Geopolitical tensions remained a key concern, impacting the global economic outlook.

items.title: Godrej Consumer's Bangladesh business returns to profit after four years led by Goodknight sales
items.summary: As Godrej Consumer’s international business turns the corner, sales of electric liquid variants of Good Knight are making Bangladesh and Sri Lanka profitable business

items.title: Manipal Hospitals is said to cut IPO valuation from $10-12 billion estimated earlier
items.summary: Still, Manipal Hospitals’ proposed listing is on track to be India’s biggest of 2026 so far, surpassing SBI Funds' $1 billion IPO this week.

items.title: Global Market: ECB weighs inflation risks, digital Euro progress at July meeting
items.summary: According to Reuters, policymakers are widely expected to keep the ECB's key interest rate unchanged at 2.25%, following June's surprise rate hike that made the central bank the first among the world's major monetary authorities to tighten policy in response to the geopolitical fallout from the Iran conflict.

items.title: CCI junks complaint against Reliance Jio Infocom, others
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) has dismissed a complaint against Reliance Jio Infocom Ltd and over 4,500 companies, citing a lack of evidence to support allegations of anti-competitive conduct. The complainant accused firms across telecom, logistics, GeM procurement, energy, FMCG, and healthcare of cartelisation and abuse of dominance.

items.title: Expert view: Indian stock market to deliver healthy double-digit returns over medium term, says Bajaj Life's CIO
items.summary: Bajaj Life's CIO Srinivas Rao Ravuri forecasts promising double-digit returns for Indian equities over the medium term, emphasising improved earnings visibility and attractive valuations. Amid geopolitical fluctuations, he advocates for a balanced investment approach across market capitalisations.

items.title: No Smoke, Only Water Vapour: PM Modi flags off India’s first hydrogen train
Why the Strait of Hormuz is losing importance
items.summary: The Iran-Israel conflict briefly raised fears that disruption of the Strait of Hormuz, through which about 20% of global oil and gas passes, would trigger a sharp energy crisis. Instead, oil markets remained resilient due to ample global supplies, coordinated releases of oil reserves, limited buying by China, and increased use of alternative export pipelines.

items.title: Vedanta, demerged Vedanta Aluminium, Vedanta Oil & Gas get rating upgrades from CRISIL
items.summary: CRISIL has upgraded ratings for Vedanta Aluminium Metal and Vedanta Oil and Gas. These upgrades reflect stronger business and financial risk profiles for the demerged entities. Hindustan Zinc Ltd continues to be fully consolidated with Vedanta due to strong integration. Vedanta's financial risk profile has improved significantly after the demerger. Ongoing exploration programs are expected to support production sustainability and reserve replacement.

items.title: Federal Bank Q1 Results: Profit jumps 36.5% YoY to ₹1,177 crore
items.summary: Federal Bank Q1 Results: Federal Bank's Q1 FY27 results show a 36.5% rise in net profit to ₹1,177 crore, driven by strong net interest income growth of 26%. Asset quality improved with gross NPAs at 1.52% and net NPAs at a record low of 0.18%.

items.title: BP, ConocoPhillips to back Iraq with major investments as U.S. seeks to weaken Iran's energy hold
items.summary: BP and ConocoPhillips are set to announce billions of dollars of new investments in Iraq on Friday.

items.title: Manipal Hospitals said to cut valuation to $8.3 billion in IPO
items.summary: The Temasek Holdings-backed company is planning to raise as much as ₹11,000 crore through a share sale that’s likely to be launched in the week starting July 27

items.title: SoftBank sinks over 9% as Asia chip stocks track Wall Street AI sell-off
items.summary: The declines followed another weak session for U.S. technology stocks, with the Nasdaq Composite falling 1.47% as semiconductor shares came under renewed pressure.

items.title: FIIs cut stakes in 12 smallcap stocks in June quarter, shares slide up to 50%. Do you own any?
items.summary: FIIs trimmed stakes in 268 smallcap stocks during the June quarter, with several witnessing steep CY2026 declines. The analysis highlights top laggards facing foreign investor selling pressure.

items.title: Global Market: How leveraged ETFs are amplifying the AI chip trading frenzy
items.summary: Leveraged ETFs tracking AI chipmakers Samsung and SK Hynix have surged in South Korea. The SK Hynix-linked fund's assets have grown twentyfold this year, prompting regulators to curb promotional activity amid sharp volatility spikes tied to daily rebalancing.

items.title: Netflix says Indian titles logged 688 million views globally in H1 2026
items.summary: Indian films and series garnered 688 million views on Netflix during early 2026. This period marked the streaming platform's strongest first half for Indian content. Four Indian films reached Netflix's Global Top 100 Films list. Taskaree: The Smuggler's Web ranked highest among Indian series globally. Netflix plans continued investment in expanding Indian stories' global reach.

items.title: Gold futures drop to ₹1,40,100/10g
items.summary: On MCX, yellow metal contracts for August delivery traded lower by ₹248, or 0.18 per cent, at ₹1,40,100 per 10 grams in a business turnover of 1,026 lots.

items.title: DS Group launches wearable safety filter for women farmers in Uttar Pradesh
items.summary: DS Group introduced protective filters for women farmers in Uttar Pradesh. These nano-fibre filters are designed to be stitched into traditional attire. items.title: India-UK CETA takes effect: First zero-duty Indian coffee, jewellery consignments reach UK shores
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement has officially come into force. Zero-duty consignments of Indian jewellery and coffee have now landed in the United Kingdom. This landmark pact is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade between the two nations. Businesses anticipate substantial growth and increased market competitiveness following the agreement. The pact aims to strengthen supply chains and support job creation across both countries.

This pact is designed to create vast business prospects, with 93% of Indian exports obtaining duty-free entry into the EU market. Luxury items such as cars and wines will become more affordable for consumers in India.

Stricter regulations for delayed birth and death registrations are also planned. A major overhaul of higher education will dissolve existing regulatory bodies into one commission.

The value for March 2026 reached 70.0, up from 67.0 in March 2025. Growth was observed across all sub-indices, reflecting deepening financial inclusion. Usage of financial services significantly contributed to this overall improvement.

Vietnamese rice prices are unchanged as buyers await increased harvest supplies.

Marketing support will be provided to help access national and international markets.
